input.prompt: I need to convert this entire repository to Central Package Management. The solution is at advanced-multi-complexity/Enterprise.slnx. There are some complications: package versions defined as MSBuild properties, conditional package references for multi-targeting, and a shared Common.props that adds a package to all projects. Inline MSBuild property values and use the highest version for any conflicts.

data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me analyze:

- **MSBuild properties**: `$(BlobsVersion)=12.24.0`, `$(HostingVersion)=8.0.1` → inline these
- **Version conflicts**: `System.Text.Json` (8.0.5/9.0.0/10.0.1→10.0.1), `Azure.Storage.Blobs` (12.19.0/12.24.0→12.24.0), `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` (6.0.36/8.0.11→8.0.11)
- **Conditional TFM references** in Api.csproj: both conditions reference same package, so merge after unifying version
- **Common.props**: shared `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` needs version removed too

<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="12.24.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me analyze:

**MSBuild Properties to inline:**
- `$(BlobsVersion)` = `12.24.0` (defined in Directory.Build.props) → used in Api.csproj for `Azure.Storage.Blobs`
- `$(HostingVersion)` = `8.0.1` (defined in Directory.Build.props) → used in Core.csproj for `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting`

**Package versions across all projects (including Common.props):**

| Package | Source | Version |
|---|---|---|
| Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | Common.props | 9.0.0 |


| System.Text.Json | Api.csproj | 10.0.1 |
| System.Text.Json | Core.csproj | 9.0.0 |
| System.Text.Json | Legacy.csproj | 8.0.5 |
| System.Text.Json | Web.csproj | 10.0.1 |
| System.Text.Json | Tests.csproj | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Storage.Blobs | Api.csproj | 12.24.0 |

I'm seeing System.Text.Json has inconsistent versions across projects, with Core and Legacy on older versions while the others are on 10.0.1. Azure.Storage.Blobs is currently referenced through the MSBuild property.

Looking at the rest of the dependencies, there are multiple frameworks in play for AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson across net6.0 and net8.0, along with hosting and telemetry packages. The version conflict analysis shows System.Text.Json needs to be resolved to 10.0.1 as the highest version across all projects.

For Azure.Storage.Blobs, I'm choosing 12.24.0 as the newer version. With AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson though, I'm running into a constraint—the package has different versions for different target frameworks, but Central Package Management only allows one version per package. The user said to use the highest version, which would be 8.0.11, though this could create compatibility issues for the net6.0 target that originally needed 6.0.36.

I'm going to follow the user's instruction and go with 8.0.11. Now I need to create the Directory.Packages.props file with all the centralized package versions, then remove the version attributes from every PackageReference across the project files. Finishing the package versions list and then updating the project files to remove the now-redundant version properties from Directory.Build.props and Common.props... Finishing the Api.csproj configuration...

I'm realizing that since both conditional ItemGroups reference the same package with no version differences anymore, I could consolidate them into a single unconditional ItemGroup—the package would be included for both net6.0 and net8.0 anyway. But looking back at the original setup, these conditionals existed because different versions were required per target framework, so I need to reconsider whether that's still the case here. The conditional structure made sense when different versions were needed per target framework, but since CPM enforces a single version across all frameworks, consolidating to one ItemGroup is the right move. Now I'm showing what the simplified project files look like for Core and Legacy. Now I'm setting up the test project with xunit and the same JSON library, targeting .NET 8.0 as well. Let me go ahead and implement all these project configurations.

data.content: Let me also verify no `Version=` attributes remain on any PackageReference:

All changes are complete. Here's a summary:

**Created** `Directory.Packages.props` with 7 centralized package versions:
- Resolved `System.Text.Json` → **10.0.1** (from 8.0.5, 9.0.0, 10.0.1)
- Resolved `Azure.Storage.Blobs` → **12.24.0** (from 12.19.0, `$(BlobsVersion)`=12.24.0)
- Resolved `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` → **8.0.11** (from 6.0.36, 8.0.11)

**Modified 7 files:**
- **Directory.Build.props** — removed `BlobsVersion`/`HostingVersion` properties (inlined)
- **Common.props** — removed `Version` from shared `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging`
- **Api.csproj** — removed versions, inlined `$(BlobsVersion)`, merged two conditional TFM ItemGroups into one (same version now)
- **Core.csproj** — removed versions, inlined `$(HostingVersion)`
- **Legacy.csproj**, **Web.csproj**, **Tests.csproj** — removed versions
